brief.query: Generate and validate a vtk.js volume rendering scene with browser evidence vtk.js Volume Benchmark The candidate should preserve volume-specific setup hints while staying browser-verifiable.
recommendedPatterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval.
Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Retrieve mapper plus transfer-function setup before generating volume scenes.
Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order.
failureFixPairs: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
If volume output is blank, verify transfer-function and camera-reset steps before deeper repair.
If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
brief.generationPrompt: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: volume. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Retrieve mapper plus transfer-function setup before generating volume scenes. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | If volume output is blank, verify transfer-function and camera-reset steps before deeper repair. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated volume workflow should make mapper plus transfer-function setup explicit..
acceptanceChecks: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors.
The generated volume workflow should make mapper plus transfer-function setup explicit.

generatedCode.summary: vtk.js generated candidate for volume scene

starterNotes: This generated candidate keeps a deterministic browser-validation path by default.
The browser script is ready for Phase 5 render verification without additional bundling steps.
The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
